title: StatLeanAgent Progressive Build Blueprint
target: Build a verifier-first, human-guided AI system that progressively grows a Lean statistical inference library, benchmarks proof capability, and prepares auditable SFT/DPO/GRPO training data.
loop_contract: Run the smoke/eval path when the tree is clean or after local changes.
Inspect this blueprint and select the first non-done phase and milestone.
If health is green, implement or evaluate the next unblocked milestone instead of only monitoring.
Promote changes only after Python tests, deterministic benchmark regeneration, Lean build or CI, and no forbidden Lean shortcuts.
Use AXLE only as an auxiliary Lean Engine for extraction, checking, proof repair, theorem-hole generation, and signature validation; local Lake remains the acceptance authority.
Update this blueprint when a milestone changes status.
promotion_gates: No accepted sorry/admit/unsafe/unreviewed axiom in StatInference.
Premise index and training manifest can be regenerated.
Strict GitHub CI Lean lake build passes.
Any AXLE-generated or AXLE-repaired Lean code is rechecked locally and does not weaken the source theorem statement.
Statistical meaning of new definitions is human-reviewable and non-vacuity is tracked.

phases.name: Green Verifier Baseline
phases.objective: Create the repository, strict CI, smoke path, seed Lean modules, benchmark registry, premise indexing, and training manifest scaffold.
evidence: commit 4d5474a
CI run 25199578921 success
45 Python tests
27 seed benchmark tasks
199 indexed local Lean premises

milestones.name: Strict Python and Lean CI
Seed StatInference modules
Deterministic StatInferBench seed registry
Premise indexing and training manifest scaffold

phases.name: Statistics Core Theorem Layer
phases.objective: Move from bridge interfaces toward reusable concrete theorem families for asymptotic statistics and statistical learning.

milestones.name: ERM deterministic oracle/excess-risk inequalities
Convergence bridge interfaces for Slutsky, delta method, CLT route
M-estimator and Z-estimator deterministic oracle interfaces
Mathlib-backed convergence wrappers for convergence in probability/distribution
evidence: StatInference.Asymptotics.Convergence defines MathlibConvergesInProbability and MathlibConvergesInDistribution on top of mathlib TendstoInMeasure/TendstoInDistribution.
StatInference.mathlib_convergesInDistribution_of_probability wraps mathlib's convergence-in-probability implies convergence-in-distribution theorem.
benchmark mathlib_probability_to_distribution_seed accepted locally

milestones.name: Finite-class uniform convergence theorem and ERM consistency corollary
evidence: StatInference.EmpiricalProcess.Basic defines oracle_ineq_of_uniform_deviation_on and oracle_excess_sequence_bound_on for restricted classes.
StatInference.FiniteClassUniformConvergence packages finite-class uniform deviation plus a vanishing radius.
FiniteClassUniformConvergence.eventually_excessRisk_lt_of_approx_erm proves eventual excess-risk consistency for approximate ERM.
benchmarks finite_class_oracle_excess_on_seed and finite_class_erm_consistency_seed accepted locally

milestones.name: Concrete ratio/Hajek/IPW estimator algebra skeleton
evidence: StatInference.Estimator.Ratio defines ratioEstimate, ratioResidual, RatioEstimatorSequence, hajekRatio, and hajekResidual.
StatInference.ratio_sub_target_eq_residual_div proves the deterministic ratio linearization identity.
StatInference.scaled_hajekRatio_sub_target_eq_residual_div proves the scaled Hajek/IPW residual identity for asymptotic-rate handoff.
benchmarks ratio_error_identity_seed and scaled_hajek_ratio_error_seed accepted locally

next_actions: P1 is complete; continue to empirical process notation and GC modules in P2.

phases.name: Empirical Process And GC Library
phases.objective: Build empirical measures, finite-class GC, covering-number interfaces, and the first reusable Glivenko-Cantelli theorem family.

milestones.name: Empirical average and empirical risk notation
evidence: StatInference.EmpiricalProcess.Average defines empiricalAverage, empiricalRiskOfLoss, indexedEmpiricalRiskOfLoss, and empiricalRiskSequenceOfSamples.
StatInference.empiricalRiskSequence_excess_bound_of_uniform_deviation connects loss-based empirical risk notation to the deterministic ERM excess-risk theorem.
benchmarks empirical_average_unfold_seed and empirical_risk_sequence_oracle_seed accepted locally

milestones.name: Finite union/finite class uniform deviation bound interface
evidence: StatInference.EmpiricalProcess.Finite defines DeviationFailureEventOn as the finite-union failure event for restricted-class deviations.
StatInference.empiricalDeviationBoundOn_of_not_deviationFailureEventOn converts no-failure evidence into EmpiricalDeviationBoundOn.
StatInference.FiniteUnionDeviationSequence packages finite-class no-failure certificates and converts them into EmpiricalDeviationSequenceOn.
StatInference.FiniteUnionDeviationSequence.excessRiskBound feeds the finite-union certificate into the approximate-ERM excess-risk theorem.
benchmarks finite_union_no_failure_seed and finite_union_sequence_excess_seed accepted locally

milestones.name: GC class preservation for subclasses/projections
evidence: StatInference.EmpiricalProcess.Preservation defines FiniteClassMarker.project for finite subclasses.
DeviationFailureEventOn.mono and not_of_not_larger preserve finite-union failure/no-failure evidence under subclass projection.
FiniteUnionDeviationCertificate.project and FiniteUnionDeviationSequence.project reuse finite-union certificates on subclasses.
FiniteClassUniformConvergence.project and projectToGlivenkoCantelliClass preserve finite-class GC certificates under subclass projection.
benchmarks finite_class_gc_projection_seed and finite_union_projection_seed accepted locally

milestones.name: Covering-number and Rademacher-compatible benchmark tasks
evidence: StatInference.EmpiricalProcess.Complexity defines CoveringNumberSpec and CoveringNumberDeviationCertificate as proof-carrying entropy-route interfaces.
StatInference.EmpiricalProcess.Complexity defines RademacherComplexitySpec and RademacherDeviationCertificate with radius = 2 * complexity + slack.
RademacherDeviationCertificate.radius_tendsto_zero proves the deterministic vanishing-radius handoff from vanishing complexity and slack.
benchmarks covering_certificate_gc_seed and rademacher_certificate_gc_seed accepted locally

next_actions: P2 is complete; continue to the asymptotic estimator library in P3.

phases.name: Asymptotic Estimator Library
phases.objective: Connect asymptotic linearity, CLT, Slutsky, M/Z-estimation, and influence expansion into reusable estimator proof paths.

milestones.name: Asymptotic linearity plus CLT theorem with explicit assumptions
evidence: StatInference.Estimator.Normality defines IndexedAsymptoticLinearCLTRoute with explicit estimator expansion, CLT, negligible remainder, and normality bridge fields.
IndexedAsymptoticLinearCLTRoute.asymptoticLinear extracts asymptotic linearity from expansion matching, expansion proof, and negligible remainder.
IndexedAsymptoticLinearCLTRoute.asymptoticNormal proves expansion + negligible remainder + CLT imply the route's asymptotic-normality statement.
benchmarks indexed_asymptotic_linear_clt_route_seed and indexed_asymptotic_linear_clt_bridge_seed accepted locally

milestones.name: Z-estimator differentiability/linearization bridge
evidence: StatInference.Estimator.ZLinearization defines ZEstimatorLinearizationBridge with explicit differentiability, estimating-equation, moment-linearization, expansion, and negligible-remainder assumptions.
ZEstimatorLinearizationBridge.toIndexedAsymptoticLinearEstimator projects the Z-estimator route into the generic indexed asymptotic-linear estimator interface.
ZEstimatorAsymptoticNormalRoute.asymptoticNormal composes the Z-linearization bridge with the P3.M1 CLT normality route.
benchmarks z_estimator_linearization_bridge_seed and z_estimator_asymptotic_normal_route_seed accepted locally

milestones.name: M-estimator argmin consistency bridge
evidence: StatInference.Estimator.MConsistency defines MEstimatorArgminConsistencyRoute with explicit sample path, uniform-deviation convergence, and approximate-argmin tolerance convergence assumptions.
MEstimatorArgminConsistencyRoute.excessRiskBound projects the deterministic M-estimator oracle inequality along the stored sample path.
MEstimatorArgminConsistencyRoute.eventually_excessRisk_lt proves eventual excess-risk consistency from vanishing deviation radius and tolerance.
benchmarks m_estimator_argmin_consistency_bound_seed and m_estimator_argmin_consistency_eventual_seed accepted locally

milestones.name: Delta-method theorem family for estimator transformations
evidence: StatInference.Estimator.DeltaMethod defines ParameterTransformation and DeltaMethodEstimatorRoute for transformed estimators.
DeltaMethodEstimatorRoute.toDeltaMethodBridge exposes estimator transformations as the generic delta-method bridge.
DeltaMethodEstimatorRoute.transformedConvergence proves transformed-estimator convergence from the transform relation, linear-part convergence, and negligible remainder.
benchmarks delta_method_estimator_bridge_seed and delta_method_estimator_convergence_seed accepted locally

next_actions: P3 is complete; continue to causal and semiparametric inference in P4.

phases.name: Causal And Semiparametric Inference
phases.objective: Build potential outcomes, IPW/AIPW/TMLE-style bridges, influence functions, and Neyman orthogonality with explicit assumptions.

milestones.name: ATE identification sanity and non-vacuity examples
evidence: StatInference.Causal.ATE defines DeterministicATECase as a concrete one-unit real-valued potential-outcome model.
DeterministicATECase.observedATE_identified proves the observed ATE bridge conclusion from verified consistency, overlap, and unconfoundedness certificates.
ATEIdentificationSanityExample.ofOutcomes constructs a non-vacuous inhabited ATE identification witness from two real outcomes.
benchmarks ate_identification_sanity_witness_seed and ate_identification_sanity_verified_seed accepted locally

milestones.name: IPW/Hajek ratio estimator linearization
evidence: StatInference.Causal.IPW defines VerifiedIPWIdentification as a proof-carrying certificate over consistency, overlap, unconfoundedness, correct propensity weights, and finite weight moments.
IPWHajekEstimatorSequence.sub_target_eq_residual_div and scaled_sub_target_eq_residual_div lift the deterministic ratio residual identities to IPW/Hajek sequences.
IPWHajekLinearizationRoute combines a verified IPW identification certificate with denominator nonzero evidence and exposes linearization and scaledLinearization theorems.
constantIPWHajekLinearizationRoute constructs a concrete non-vacuity witness with one-mass weights.
benchmarks ipw_identification_certificate_seed, ipw_hajek_scaled_linearization_route_seed, constant_ipw_hajek_route_seed, and constant_ipw_hajek_exact_target_seed accepted locally

milestones.name: AIPW orthogonality interface and product-rate bridge
evidence: StatInference.Causal.AIPW defines VerifiedAIPWDoubleRobustIdentification as a proof-carrying double-robust identification certificate.
AIPWNuisanceProductRate and VerifiedAIPWNuisanceProductRate package product-rate nuisance control and extract the small-o second-order remainder.
AIPWOrthogonalProductRateRoute combines AIPW identification, verified Neyman orthogonality, and product-rate control into secondOrderRemainderSmall.
trivialAIPWOrthogonalProductRateRoute constructs a concrete non-vacuity witness for the AIPW orthogonality/product-rate API.

benchmarks aipw_double_robust_identification_seed, aipw_product_rate_remainder_seed, aipw_orthogonal_score_seed, aipw_second_order_remainder_seed, and trivial_aipw_product_rate_route_seed accepted locally

milestones.name: Influence-function asymptotic normality route
evidence: StatInference.Semiparametric.Normality defines InfluenceFunctionNormalityRoute from verified influence functions, target/regularity certificates, CLT, and negligible-remainder obligations to asymptotic normality.
InfluenceFunctionNormalityRoute.toAsymptoticLinearCLTBridge exposes the semiparametric route as the generic asymptotic-linearity plus CLT bridge.
AIPWInfluenceFunctionNormalityRoute composes AIPW influence-function verification with the generic semiparametric normality route.
trivialInfluenceFunctionNormalityRoute and trivialAIPWInfluenceFunctionNormalityRoute provide concrete non-vacuity witnesses.

benchmarks influence_function_normality_route_seed, influence_function_normality_bridge_seed, aipw_influence_function_normality_route_seed, trivial_influence_function_normality_seed, and trivial_aipw_influence_function_normality_seed accepted locally

next_actions: P4 is complete; continue to proof-agent and benchmark loop hardening in P5.

phases.name: Proof Agent And Benchmark Loop
phases.objective: Turn the library into a measurable proof-agent loop with theorem-hole tasks, repair tasks, premise-use metrics, and failure taxonomy.

milestones.name: Benchmark tasks beyond projection examples
evidence: benchmarks include subgoal_completion theorem-hole tasks ipw_linearization_theorem_hole_seed, aipw_product_rate_theorem_hole_seed, and if_normality_theorem_hole_seed.
Each theorem-hole task has explicit proof_state metadata, expected premises, multi-goal constructor structure, and lean_task.allowed_sorry scoped to the benchmark skeleton.
tests/test_benchmarks.py enforces that theorem-hole tasks are explicit, policy-scoped, and carry proof_state plus expected_premises metadata.
benchmarks/README.md documents theorem-hole tasks and clarifies that placeholders are allowed only in benchmark skeletons, not in StatInference.
the three theorem-hole seeds accepted locally with allowed placeholder diagnostics

milestones.name: Verifier artifacts for all benchmark tasks
evidence: artifacts/verification/benchmark-seed-reports.jsonl regenerated from the current 67-task benchmark registry.
verify-benchmarks accepted all 67 seed tasks with the local Lake verifier.
The theorem-hole tasks record allowed placeholder diagnostics while non-placeholder StatInference tasks remain accepted without placeholder diagnostics.
tests/test_benchmarks.py now enforces verifier artifact coverage, accepted status, task ordering, and placeholder-policy diagnostics for checked-in reports.

milestones.name: Eval summaries by phase/domain/split
evidence: materialize-benchmark-attempts CLI renders all 67 benchmark seeds into ProofAttempt JSONL records with expected-premise metadata.
eval-summary CLI can now write JSON summary artifacts as well as stdout summaries.
artifacts/evaluation/benchmark-seed-attempts.jsonl and benchmark-seed-summary.json were generated from the verified seed registry.
The checked-in summary reports 67/67 accepted attempts, phase rows P1-P5, split rows train/dev/test, domain rows, and task-type rows including three subgoal_completion theorem-hole tasks.
tests/test_evaluation.py enforces that checked-in eval artifacts cover the current seed registry and include phase/task-type coverage.

milestones.name: Curated lemma growth ledger
evidence: CuratedLemmaLedgerEntry contract records candidate, decision, source tasks, verifier report ids, status, and notes.
build-lemma-ledger CLI generates theorem-hole-derived curation records from benchmark tasks plus verifier reports.
artifacts/curation/theorem-hole-ledger.jsonl seeds three reusable lemma candidates from the IPW, AIPW product-rate, and influence-function theorem-hole tasks.
All three seed ledger entries are status=blocked_placeholder because the curation gate detects scoped `sorry` placeholders and refuses promotion until no-sorry proofs exist.
schemas/curated_lemma_ledger.schema.json documents the ledger artifact shape and tests enforce blocked-placeholder behavior.

next_actions: P5 is complete; continue to P6 domain adaptation training data, starting with SFT examples from verified proof traces.

phases.name: Domain Adaptation Training Pipeline
phases.objective: Prepare auditable SFT, DPO, and GRPO data generation from verified traces and Lean-labeled attempts.

milestones.name: SFT examples from verified proof traces
evidence: build-training-manifest CLI now accepts paired ProofAttempt and VerificationReport artifacts.
build_verified_sft_examples emits accepted no-placeholder SFT examples from verifier-backed proof attempts.
The generated artifacts/training/manifest.json uses sft_source=verified_attempts, 67 paired attempts, 64 no-placeholder SFT examples, and 67 GRPO tasks.
The three theorem-hole tasks are deliberately excluded from SFT because lean_task.allowed_sorry=true.
tests/test_training.py enforces checked-in verified-SFT manifest counts and absence of `sorry` in SFT responses.

milestones.name: DPO pairs from accepted/rejected Lean attempts
evidence: materialize-dpo-rejections CLI creates deterministic same-statement rejected attempts for each no-placeholder benchmark task.
verify-attempts CLI verifies arbitrary ProofAttempt JSONL records and produced 64/64 rejected Lean-labeled DPO negative reports.
Verifier diagnostics now sanitize temporary source paths to stable Task.lean locations for deterministic rejected-report artifacts.
artifacts/training/dpo-negative-attempts.jsonl and dpo-negative-reports.jsonl contain 64 generated unknown-premise failures without `sorry` or `admit`.
artifacts/training/manifest.json now contains 64 DPO chosen/rejected pairs, 64 verified no-placeholder SFT examples, and 67 GRPO tasks.
tests/test_training.py enforces checked-in DPO artifact counts, rejected labels, sanitized diagnostics, and DPO pair manifest metadata.

milestones.name: Process-reward GRPO task manifest
evidence: GRPOProcessTask records reward source, rendered Lean code, verifier command, allowed placeholder policy, forbidden tokens, expected premises, reward components, split, task type, and domain tags.
materialize-grpo-tasks CLI generated artifacts/training/grpo-process-tasks.jsonl with 67 process-reward tasks.
The artifact marks exactly three theorem-hole tasks with allowed_placeholders=["sorry"] and all other tasks with no allowed placeholders.
Each GRPO process task encodes a reproducible verifier command through statlean_agent.cli verify-task with the benchmark registry and local Lake repo.
schemas/grpo_process_task.schema.json documents the process-task artifact and tests enforce reward-policy/verifier metadata.

milestones.name: Baseline prover comparison on held-out StatInferBench
evidence: compare_baseline_on_split records held-out split metrics, per-task effective verifier status, reward components, failure category, expected-premise recall, and allowed-placeholder policy.
baseline-comparison CLI generated artifacts/evaluation/heldout-baseline.json for the seed-registry baseline on the test split.
The checked-in held-out report covers 2/2 test tasks, passes 2/2, reports pass_rate=1.0, mean_premise_recall=1.0, and contains no failure categories.
schemas/baseline_comparison.schema.json documents the baseline comparison artifact shape and tests enforce checked-in held-out coverage.

next_actions: P6 is complete; continue to curator-gated progressive library growth in P7.

phases.name: Progressive Library Growth
phases.objective: Add a curator-gated mechanism for lemma mining, duplicate detection, non-vacuity examples, and downstream-use tracking.

milestones.name: Lemma proposal schema
evidence: LemmaProposal contract records proposal id, source kind, proposer, nested candidate, source tasks, domain tags, expected premises, required gates, blocked reasons, status, and notes.
build_theorem_hole_lemma_proposals creates pre-curation proposal records from theorem-hole benchmark tasks without promoting them into StatInference.
build-lemma-proposals CLI generated artifacts/curation/lemma-proposals.jsonl with three blocked theorem-hole-derived proposals.
Each proposal requires lean_compiles, no_forbidden_tokens, no_duplicate_statement, minimal_imports, non_vacuity_example, downstream_reuse, and statistical_semantic_review gates.
schemas/lemma_proposal.schema.json documents the proposal artifact and tests enforce checked-in proposal coverage.

milestones.name: Duplicate and import-minimality checks
evidence: LemmaProposalGateReport contract records duplicate-name matches, duplicate-statement matches, imports added, required imports, unused imports, missing imports, pass/fail status, and required changes.
build_lemma_proposal_gate_reports compares proposal names/statements against current proposals and the local StatInference premise index.
Import-minimality is checked by resolving each proposal's expected premises to premise-index modules and comparing those required modules with candidate imports_added.
check-lemma-proposals CLI generated artifacts/curation/lemma-proposal-gates.jsonl with three passing proposal gate reports.
schemas/lemma_proposal_gate_report.schema.json documents the gate artifact and tests enforce duplicate/import failure behavior plus checked-in passing reports.

milestones.name: Non-vacuity example requirement
evidence: LemmaNonVacuityReport contract records proposal domains, matching non-vacuity evidence tasks, accepted evidence tasks, missing evidence, pass/fail status, and required changes.
build_lemma_non_vacuity_reports requires each proposal to match at least one benchmark tagged non_vacuity that shares a domain-specific proposal tag.
check-lemma-non-vacuity CLI generated artifacts/curation/lemma-non-vacuity.jsonl with three passing reports backed by accepted non-vacuity benchmark tasks.

The IPW proposal is backed by constant IPW/Hajek non-vacuity tasks, the AIPW product-rate proposal by AIPW non-vacuity tasks, and the influence-function proposal by influence-function non-vacuity tasks.
schemas/lemma_non_vacuity_report.schema.json documents the artifact and tests enforce passing checked-in reports plus blocked missing/rejected evidence behavior.

milestones.name: Downstream proof-cost improvement metric
evidence: LemmaProofCostReport contract records downstream task ids, expected premises, baseline/projected step counts, proof-cost delta, relative reduction, pass/fail status, and required changes.
build_lemma_proof_cost_reports requires a proposal to replace a multi-premise downstream proof bundle with a projected one-step lemma application.
check-lemma-proof-cost CLI generated artifacts/curation/lemma-proof-cost.jsonl with three passing reports and total projected proof-cost delta 3.
The three current theorem-hole proposals each replace a two-premise constructor bundle with one promoted lemma use, giving proof_cost_delta=1 and relative_cost_reduction=0.5.
schemas/lemma_proof_cost_report.schema.json documents the artifact and tests enforce passing checked-in reports plus blocked single-premise/no-downstream-use behavior.

next_actions: P7 is complete; continue to paper-quality system demonstration in P8.

phases.name: Paper-Quality System Demonstration
phases.objective: Demonstrate that the system improves statistical theorem formalization over general Lean provers and produces reusable Lean statistics infrastructure.

milestones.name: Held-out benchmark and baseline results
evidence: build_paper_quality_heldout_report composes held-out baseline comparison, failure taxonomy, and audited non-seed proof-chain coverage.
paper-quality-heldout CLI generated artifacts/evaluation/paper-quality-heldout.json for the seed-registry baseline on the test split.
The paper-quality report covers 2/2 held-out test tasks with pass_rate=1.0 and an explicit empty failure taxonomy.
The report audits three non-seed reusable StatInference theorem chains: IPW/Hajek linearization, AIPW product-rate remainder, and influence-function normality.
schemas/paper_quality_heldout.schema.json documents the report and tests enforce checked-in held-out coverage plus proof-chain pass coverage.

milestones.name: One concrete estimator proof chain
evidence: StatInference.Examples.ConcreteEstimatorChain defines paperQualityIPWHajekConcreteEstimatorChain as a no-sorry theorem composing IPW identification, exact target recovery, zero residual, and scaled linearization.
paper_quality_ipw_hajek_concrete_chain_seed was added to StatInferBench as a paper-quality concrete estimator chain benchmark.
concrete-estimator-chain-report CLI generated artifacts/evaluation/concrete-estimator-chain.json with verification_status=accepted, passed=true, and component coverage 4/4.
The benchmark registry now verifies 68/68 seed tasks and the concrete chain is included in SFT/DPO/GRPO training artifacts.
schemas/concrete_estimator_chain.schema.json documents the report and tests enforce checked-in chain proof evidence.

milestones.name: Ablations for retrieval, SFT, DPO, process reward, and curation
evidence: build_ablation_report composes P8 held-out, concrete-estimator, training, GRPO, DPO, and curation artifacts into an auditable component-ablation readiness report.
ablation-report CLI generated artifacts/evaluation/ablation-report.json with full_system_ready=true, five component rows, and six ablation variants.
The report records retrieval mean_premise_recall=1.0, verified SFT example count, DPO pair/rejected-report counts, GRPO process-task reward components, and curation gate pass counts.
The report explicitly labels the result as a system-evidence ablation scaffold rather than a trained-model performance claim.
schemas/ablation_report.schema.json documents the artifact and tests enforce checked-in ablation coverage.

milestones.name: Research paper draft and reproducibility bundle
evidence: docs/paper_draft.md captures the paper narrative, core claim boundaries, contributions, evaluation snapshot, limitations, and next research steps.
build_reproducibility_bundle records artifact sha256 hashes, validation commands, blueprint completion state, paper draft linkage, reproduction order, and guardrails.

reproducibility-bundle CLI generated artifacts/evaluation/reproducibility-bundle.json from the checked-in blueprint, paper draft, benchmark, verification, evaluation, training, and curation artifacts.
schemas/reproducibility_bundle.schema.json documents the artifact and tests enforce checked-in bundle coverage.
The reproducibility bundle marks all phases done and ties the P8 paper-quality demonstration to executable validation commands.

next_actions: Blueprint complete; continue future work by opening a new post-P8 phase for external prover baselines, theorem-hole proof promotion, or empirical-process expansion.

phases.name: External Baselines And Proof Promotion
phases.objective: Move beyond the seed-registry demonstration by preparing external prover baselines, promoting theorem-hole lemmas into no-placeholder Lean proofs, and expanding empirical-process coverage.

milestones.name: External prover baseline run plan
evidence: build_external_baseline_plan creates a concrete post-P8 baseline matrix over the current held-out StatInferBench split.

external-baseline-plan CLI generated artifacts/evaluation/external-baseline-plan.json with five baselines: seed-registry, ReProver, DeepSeek-Prover-V2 7B, Kimina-Prover-RL 1.7B, and a general coding-agent Lean baseline.
The report marks only seed-registry as ready and records model/harness requirements for the four blocked external baselines.
The report fixes target task ids, theorem-hole task ids, output paths, command templates, and metrics including pass@k, valid tactic rate, premise recall, timeout rate, and wall time.
schemas/external_baseline_plan.schema.json documents the artifact and tests enforce checked-in plan coverage.

milestones.name: Theorem-hole lemma promotion queue
evidence: StatInference.Causal.IPW now contains the no-placeholder theorem StatInference.ipw_hajek_linearization_constructor bundling route.identifies and route.scaledLinearization.
StatInference.Causal.AIPW now contains the no-placeholder theorem StatInference.aipw_product_rate_route_constructor bundling route.identifies and route.secondOrderRemainderSmall.
StatInference.Semiparametric.Normality now contains the no-placeholder theorem StatInference.influence_function_normality_route_constructor bundling route.asymptoticLinear and route.asymptoticNormal.

theorem-hole-promotion-queue CLI generated artifacts/curation/theorem-hole-promotion-queue.json ranking all theorem-hole candidates and naming ipw_linearization_theorem_hole_seed as the first promotion target.
schemas/theorem_hole_promotion_queue.schema.json documents the artifact and tests enforce promoted no-placeholder queue coverage.

milestones.name: External baseline result ingestion and comparison
evidence: build_external_baseline_results normalizes external ProofAttempt and VerificationReport JSONL files against the same held-out split used by seed-registry.

external-baseline-results CLI generated artifacts/evaluation/external-baseline-results.json, ingesting seed-registry through checked-in fallback artifacts and marking four model/agent baselines blocked until adapters produce attempt/report files.
The report records per-baseline ingestion status, pass rate, mean reward, premise recall, failure taxonomy, source artifact, and blocked reasons.
The seed-registry command template in external-baseline-plan was corrected to use verify-attempts --output for reproducible report generation.
schemas/external_baseline_results.schema.json documents the artifact and tests enforce checked-in result coverage.

milestones.name: Empirical-process expansion targets for bracketing, VC, and Donsker interfaces
evidence: StatInference.EmpiricalProcess.Complexity now defines BracketingNumberSpec and BracketingDeviationCertificate as proof-carrying bracketing routes to GlivenkoCantelliClass.
StatInference.EmpiricalProcess.Complexity now defines VCSubgraphSpec and VCDeviationCertificate as proof-carrying VC-subgraph routes to GlivenkoCantelliClass.
StatInference.EmpiricalProcess.Complexity now defines DonskerBridgeCertificate to connect verified GC classes with DonskerSpec weak-convergence statements.
empirical-process-targets CLI generated artifacts/evaluation/empirical-process-targets.json with five scoped targets: bracketing, VC-subgraph, Donsker, covering-number, and Rademacher.
schemas/empirical_process_targets.schema.json documents the target artifact and tests enforce checked-in P9.M4 target coverage.

next_actions: P9 is complete; continue to P10 empirical-process theorem expansion.

phases.name: Empirical Process Theorem Expansion
phases.objective: Turn the P9 bracketing, VC-subgraph, and Donsker interfaces into benchmarked Lean theorem targets with non-vacuity evidence and downstream statistical inference handoffs.

milestones.name: Bracketing GC benchmark seeds and non-vacuity witness
evidence: StatInference.EmpiricalProcess.Complexity now defines trivialBracketingNumberSpec as a concrete one-point bracketing metadata witness.

StatInference.EmpiricalProcess.Complexity now defines trivialBracketingDeviationCertificate and trivialBracketingGlivenkoCantelliClass as no-placeholder non-vacuity witnesses for the bracketing GC interface.
benchmarks include bracketing_certificate_gc_seed for BracketingDeviationCertificate.toGlivenkoCantelliClass.
benchmarks include trivial_bracketing_gc_non_vacuity_seed tagged bracketing_number and non_vacuity.
artifacts/evaluation/empirical-process-targets.json now marks bracketing_gc_interface as ready_for_lemma_targets with family-specific benchmark evidence.

milestones.name: VdV&W finite-bracketing GC deterministic core
evidence: AXLE is now available as an auxiliary extraction/check/repair backend through the StatLean CLI, with API keys read only from AXLE_API_KEY and local Lake retained as the verifier of record.
The VdV&W markdown audit identifies Theorem 2.4.1 as the first bottom-up empirical-process target: finite L1 bracketing implies Glivenko-Cantelli.

StatInference.EmpiricalProcess.Bracketing formalizes the deterministic bracketing inequality used in the proof of Theorem 2.4.1: bracket endpoint control plus population bracket width control implies a uniform empirical-deviation bound.
bracketing_deterministic_bound_seed benchmarks the deterministic bracketing inequality against the local Lake verifier.
StatInference.EmpiricalProcess.EndpointStrongLaw wraps mathlib's strong law for real-valued endpoint empirical averages and proves the finite-endpoint almost-sure convergence handoff.
StatInference.EmpiricalProcess.L1Bracketing defines explicit finite L1 bracketing families and converts shrinking finite-L1-bracketing sequence routes into GlivenkoCantelliClass certificates.
l1_bracketing_sequence_gc_seed benchmarks the explicit L1-bracketing route-to-GC handoff against the local Lake verifier.
finite_endpoint_strong_law_eventual_bound_seed benchmarks the mathlib strong-law wrapper that gives almost-sure eventual fixed-tolerance endpoint control.
The VdV&W audit docs record the remaining outer-probability, measurability, and convergence-mode gap before claiming the literal theorem statement.

milestones.name: VC-subgraph GC benchmark seeds and combinatorial proof obligations
evidence: StatInference.EmpiricalProcess.VCSubgraph defines explicit VCSubgraphProofObligations and VCSubgraphGCRoute scaffolding over the proof-carrying VCDeviationCertificate interface.
VCSubgraphProofObligations separates measurability, shatter coefficient, Sauer-Shelah, entropy translation, envelope, and separability obligations instead of asserting a primitive VC theorem.
trivialVCSubgraphGCRoute and trivialVCSubgraphGlivenkoCantelliClass provide a concrete one-point non-vacuity witness without sorry/admit/axiom/unsafe shortcuts.
benchmarks include vc_deviation_certificate_gc_seed, vc_subgraph_route_certificate_seed, and trivial_vc_subgraph_gc_non_vacuity_seed.
artifacts/evaluation/empirical-process-targets.json marks vc_subgraph_gc_interface as ready_for_lemma_targets with family-specific benchmark evidence.
verify-benchmarks accepted all 76 seed tasks with the local Lake verifier.

milestones.name: Donsker bridge benchmark seeds and asymptotic-normality handoff
evidence: StatInference.EmpiricalProcess.Donsker defines DonskerAsymptoticNormalityRoute, connecting a proof-carrying DonskerBridgeCertificate to the estimator CLT interface.
DonskerAsymptoticNormalityRoute.estimatorCLT converts certified Donsker weak convergence into the CLT statement consumed by IndexedAsymptoticLinearCLTRoute.
DonskerAsymptoticNormalityRoute.asymptoticNormal composes the Donsker-derived CLT with estimator expansion and negligible-remainder obligations to produce asymptotic normality.
trivialDonskerBridgeCertificate, trivialDonskerIndexedCLTRoute, and trivialDonskerAsymptoticNormalityRoute provide no-shortcut one-point non-vacuity witnesses.
benchmarks include donsker_bridge_gc_projection_seed, donsker_bridge_estimator_clt_seed, donsker_asymptotic_normality_handoff_seed, and trivial_donsker_asymptotic_normality_seed.
verify-benchmarks accepted all 80 seed tasks with the local Lake verifier.

milestones.name: Empirical-process external prover evaluation slice
evidence: build_empirical_process_external_prover_slice materializes a P10 empirical-process external prover evaluation slice over bracketing, VC-subgraph, and Donsker benchmark families.
artifacts/evaluation/empirical-process-external-slice.json records 13 family benchmark tasks: 5 bracketing, 3 VC-subgraph, and 5 Donsker tasks.
The seed-registry baseline is ingested from checked-in Lake verifier reports with pass_rate=1.0 over all 13 empirical-process slice tasks.
External ReProver, DeepSeek-Prover-V2, Kimina-Prover-RL, and general coding-agent rows remain blocked with concrete missing adapter/model/result-file reasons.
schemas/empirical_process_external_slice.schema.json documents the artifact and tests enforce checked-in slice coverage.

next_actions: P10 is complete; continue to P11 textbook-grounded empirical-process theorem promotion.

phases.name: Textbook-Grounded Empirical-Process Theorem Promotion
phases.objective: Use the local VdV&W textbook markdown and AXLE-assisted Lean extraction/checking to promote abstract empirical-process interfaces into source-linked theorem statements and proof-obligation inventories without semantic drift.

milestones.name: VdV&W theorem inventory and semantic audit slice
evidence: build_vdvw_theorem_inventory generates artifacts/research/vdvw-theorem-inventory.json from benchmarks/seeds.jsonl.
The inventory contains 7 source-linked VdV&W theorem cards for Theorems 2.4.1, 2.4.3, 2.5.2, 2.5.6, 2.6.4, 2.6.7, and 2.6.8.
Each row maps textbook anchors to current Lean declarations, benchmark seeds, missing primitives, semantic risks, and next actions.
All rows remain blocked_pending_primitives_or_review so bridge/certificate interfaces are not overclaimed as exact textbook theorems.
schemas/vdvw_theorem_inventory.schema.json, Python tests, and smoke determinism checks enforce the checked-in artifact.

milestones.name: Source-linked bracketing GC theorem statement candidates
evidence: build_vdvw_bracketing_gc_statement_candidates generates artifacts/research/vdvw-bracketing-gc-statement-candidates.json.

The artifact promotes VdV&W Theorem 2.4.1 into three guarded statement-candidate tracks: compiled dependency-minimal bridge, primitive L1 bracketing-number constructor, and exact outer-almost-sure textbook target.
Source anchors include the GC definition, Definition 2.1.6 bracketing numbers, and the Theorem 2.4.1 statement/proof sketch from local markdown.
Each candidate records target Lean names, required primitives, proof obligations, benchmark seeds, local Lake hooks, optional AXLE hooks, semantic risks, and promotion gates.
schemas/vdvw_bracketing_gc_statement_candidates.schema.json, Python tests, and smoke determinism checks enforce the checked-in artifact.

milestones.name: Source-linked VC and Donsker proof-obligation candidates
evidence: build_vdvw_vc_donsker_proof_obligations generates artifacts/research/vdvw-vc-donsker-proof-obligations.json.
The artifact covers VdV&W Theorems 2.5.2, 2.5.6, 2.6.4, 2.6.7, and 2.6.8 with five guarded proof-obligation cards.
The cards separate VC set entropy, VC-subgraph entropy, uniform-entropy Donsker, bracketing Donsker, and VC-subgraph Donsker tracks.
Each card records missing Lean primitives, proof obligations, current handoff declarations, benchmark seeds, validation hooks, semantic risks, and promotion gates.
schemas/vdvw_vc_donsker_proof_obligations.schema.json, Python tests, and smoke determinism checks enforce the checked-in artifact.

next_actions: P11 is complete; continue to P12 primitive empirical-process semantics.

phases.name: Primitive Empirical-Process Semantics
phases.objective: Move from source-linked theorem cards to Lean-level primitive semantics for empirical samples, outer convergence, bracketing numbers, and theorem-hole seeds needed to eventually prove VdV&W 2.4.1 and downstream VC/Donsker routes.

milestones.name: Primitive empirical sample and outer-convergence design artifact
evidence: build_vdvw_primitive_empirical_semantics generates artifacts/research/vdvw-primitive-empirical-semantics.json.
The artifact maps VdV&W GC, bracketing-number, finite-bracketing GC, uniform-entropy Donsker, and bracketing-Donsker anchors to six primitive semantic layers.

Primitive layers separate empirical sample averages, outer convergence, measurable L1 function classes, primitive L1 bracketing numbers, finite-bracketing GC assembly, and Donsker weak-convergence targets.

Each primitive records concrete target Lean module/signature sketches, existing compiled handoffs, theorem-card gaps, existing benchmark evidence, planned theorem-hole seed IDs, validation hooks, semantic risks, and promotion gates.
schemas/vdvw_primitive_empirical_semantics.schema.json, Python tests, smoke determinism checks, and the reproducibility bundle enforce the checked-in artifact.

milestones.name: Lean signatures for L1 bracketing-number constructor
evidence: StatInference.EmpiricalProcess.L1BracketingNumber defines proof-carrying L1BracketingNumberWitness, L1BracketingNumberFiniteAt, FiniteL1BracketingNumberAtEveryScale, and L1BracketingNumberConstructorObligations.
The primitive bracketing-number witness packages explicit finite bracket cover data instead of a bare Nat-valued cardinality shortcut.
L1BracketingNumberConstructorObligations.toSequenceRoute and toGlivenkoCantelliClass connect the new primitive constructor obligations to the existing shrinking finite-bracketing GC handoff.

Benchmarks finite_l1_bracketing_number_at_scale_seed, finite_l1_bracketing_every_scale_projection_seed, and finite_l1_bracketing_number_constructor_seed source-link the constructor layer to VdV&W Definition 2.1.6 and Theorem 2.4.1 without claiming exact outer-almost-sure semantics.

The theorem-hole promotion queue names a no-placeholder promoted proof block for finite_l1_bracketing_number_constructor_seed while curation still records the source benchmark as a scoped theorem-hole proposal.

milestones.name: Endpoint SLLN to finite-bracketing theorem-hole seeds
evidence: StatInference.EmpiricalProcess.VdVW241 introduces FiniteBracketEndpointStrongLawAssembly for finite bracket endpoint absolute-error control.
FiniteBracketEndpointStrongLawAssembly.toConstructorObligations converts endpoint absolute-error bounds into L1BracketingNumberConstructorObligations.
FiniteBracketEndpointStrongLawAssembly.toGlivenkoCantelliClass builds the current GlivenkoCantelliClass interface while preserving the exact outer-almost-sure theorem as a blocked semantic layer.
Benchmarks finite_bracket_endpoint_control_constructor_seed, finite_bracket_endpoint_control_gc_seed, and vdvw_2_4_1_current_gc_bridge_seed exercise the endpoint-to-constructor handoff.
FiniteBracketSampleAverageSemantics ties lower and upper endpoint empirical values to empiricalAverage over a common finite-sample sequence before forgetting into the endpoint assembly layer.
Benchmarks finite_bracket_sample_average_assembly_seed, finite_bracket_sample_average_constructor_seed, and finite_bracket_sample_average_gc_seed exercise the concrete sample-average-to-GC handoff.

FiniteBracketEmpiricalMeasureSemantics ties endpoint empirical values to empirical-measure integrals and population endpoint fields to population-measure integrals before forgetting into endpoint assembly.

StatInference.EmpiricalProcess.Outer adds outer probability, outer convergence in probability, outer almost-sure convergence, supremum-deviation, and outer-GC certificate signatures without claiming exact VdV&W 2.4.1.

FiniteBracketSampleEmpiricalMeasureSemantics adds a proof-carrying bridge requiring empirical-measure endpoint integrals to agree with sample-average endpoints before promotion to the measure-backed GC handoff.

Benchmarks finite_bracket_sample_empirical_measure_assembly_seed, finite_bracket_sample_empirical_measure_constructor_seed, and finite_bracket_sample_empirical_measure_gc_seed exercise the sample/empirical-measure bridge.

next_actions: Continue P12.M3 from the l1 bracketing-number constructor and proof-carrying sample/empirical-measure bridge toward concrete iid empirical-measure construction and endpoint SLLN event assembly while keeping the exact outer-almost-sure VdV&W 2.4.1 theorem blocked until outer convergence constructors and non-vacuity examples compile.
